_README: Per-axis contributor map. Each axis has: (1) feature contributors with signed weights, (2) archetype-anchor blend weight, (3) prose blend weight. Sum of (anchorWeight + proseWeight + featureWeight = 1) where featureWeight is implicit (1 - anchorWeight - proseWeight). Feature `weight` is the sign and prior magnitude — actual coefficients come from the calibrator (axisRegressions in scoringCalibration.json) at runtime. The `weight` here is used only when calibration coefficients are absent (cold start) or as a sign-constraint sanity check. `required` features must be present for a 'full' dataMode; missing required → dataMode degrades to 'partial' or 'scout_anchored'.

_schema.axisKey: One of: initiate, extend, close, space, connect, contain, disrupt, switch, transition. (translate is derived, not in this file.)
_schema.features: Array of {feature, weight, required, sign} contributor entries.
_schema.archetypeAnchorWeight: 0..1, fraction of final score from archetype anchor.

connect._note: Hardest axis to fit from box stats (R^2 ~0.07-0.12). Archetype anchor and authored prose carry more weight here.

contain._note: College defensive box stats barely predict NBA D (R^2 ~0.04-0.10). Anchor weight bumped to 0.30. dbpm is OPTIONAL because pre-2011 historicals don't have it; without this relaxation 48% of the historical pool would get null DefensiveVersatility grades.

disrupt._note: dbpm is OPTIONAL because pre-2011 historicals don't have it; stlPct + blkPct (universal since 1973) carry the required gate.

switch._label: Defensive scheme range, switch-ability across positions
switch._note: Anchor weight 0.30 because switchability is hard to capture without play-by-play. dbpm is OPTIONAL — see contain note for rationale.

translate._label: Meta-translation factor — how does the whole game project to the NBA
translate._derivation: Computed in src/grading/axisScores.ts as a weighted blend of (initiate + close + switch) axis scores plus stat-side modifiers (BPM, age curve). NOT a first-class axis with its own contributors.
